title: excoecaria laotica
intro: Excoecaria laotica, a plant species commonly known as the "Yellow Oleander", is a type of toxic plant that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever and inflammation, due to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. Excoecaria laotica has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and rheumatism, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
